release_notes: Stick-figure animation polish + Windows installer fix. (1) Feet no longer stick out below the Ready button during the emerge phase — _emerge_position now lifts the hip upward in proportion to leg length minus button half-height, so feet land exactly at the button's bottom edge when emerged instead of dipping below it mid-rotation. (2) Animations no longer march off the left edge of the window. The old hard-coded pixel offsets (side*290, side*340 etc) were tuned for the picker where the mock button sat right-of-center; in the live app with a centered Ready button they pushed waypoints past x=0. Replaced all per-animation spot math with a new _wander_xy helper that anchors waypoints as fractions of the available button-to-canvas-edge distance, with a 60px limb-extension margin. (3) Consecutive Ready clicks now land in different spots. AnimationPlayer stamps a per-play seed on the button dict; _pick_emerge_side, _y_jitter, and _frac_jitter derive stable per-play randomness from it, so each animation picks a different side (when room allows) and shifts spots by ±20-30px y and ±5-7% x — but stays consistent across all frames of one play. (4) Windows installer was missing ready_animations.py and ready_animation_picker.py from its supportFile copy list — fresh Windows installs since 1.0.41 had no animation module at all, so every Ready click silently no-op'd the whimsy. Now copied to %LOCALAPPDATA%\ChairsideReadyAlert\ alongside chairside_ready_alert.py. Existing Windows installs that auto-updated to 1.0.48+ will receive ready_animations.py via the manifest on next update check; installs stuck on 1.0.40 or earlier need a manual re-run of the installer or a manual copy of the file.
